trade.summary: Malaysia does not maintain diplomatic or formal trade relations with Israel. Malaysian citizens cannot travel to Israel using their passports. Any trade that occurs is indirect and minimal, routed through third countries. Malaysia is one of the most openly anti-Israel countries in Southeast Asia.

diplomatic.summary: Malaysia does not recognize Israel and has no diplomatic relations. Malaysian officials regularly condemn Israeli actions in the strongest terms. Following the 2023 Gaza conflict, Malaysia intensified its BDS campaign and called for international accountability for Israeli leaders. Malaysia uses its OIC membership to coordinate anti-Israel resolutions.

military.summary: No military relations. Malaysia views Israel as a state engaging in aggression and has called for arms embargoes. Any Israeli military presence in the region is viewed adversarially.

financial.summary: No formal financial ties. Malaysian financial institutions comply with informal BDS practices. No bilateral investment treaties or financial agreements.

energy.summary: No bilateral energy trade or cooperation.

scientific.summary: No formal scientific cooperation. Some individual-level academic contacts exist through third-country institutions but are not officially sanctioned.
